summary:
Doubling the length of a spring results in its restorative forces being approximately four times greater under ideal conditions described by Hooke’s law; however, this assumes unchanged material properties which might not always hold true practically or contextually depending upon specific scenarios provided within any exam setting constraints. The answer choice reflecting such an increase most accurately corresponds as 'four times' based primarily from fundamental mechanical laws applied directly herein. Thus, it can be concluded that when you double the length of a spring, there will indeed result roughly ‘4’ fold change impacting resultant tension/force exerted back against external stretching efforts made initially causing elongation observed earlier during initial compression/stretching cycles performed experimentally testing said object physically modeled mathematically via aforementioned equation framework utilized throughout classical mechanics education curricula globally today still widely applicable even after centuries post discovery! To simplify for clarity though : Answer remains boxed at **\[B]** indicating Four Times Increase!
